fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Kentucky Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Kentucky - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity kentucky, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to kentucky, respiratory therapist compact kentucky, kentucky respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Kentucky Board of Respiratory Care
board.mailing_address:
Kentucky Board of Respiratory Care
P.O. Box 1360
Frankfort, KY 40602

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Kentucky.

steps.title: Create a Kentucky Online Gateway (KOG) Account
steps.description: Register and create a new account on the Kentucky Online Gateway at gateway.ky.gov if you don't already have one. This account is essential for accessing and submitting your license application.

steps.title: Complete Online Application and Pay Fee
steps.description: Log in to your KOG account, select the 'Board of Respiratory Care,' and proceed with completing the 'Application for Licensure by Endorsement.' Pay the non-refundable application fee of $100 directly through the portal.

steps.title: Arrange for License Verification
steps.description: Request official license verification from all states where you have ever held a respiratory therapy license. This can often be done through the NURSYS system or by contacting the respective state boards directly to have verifications sent to the Kentucky Board of Respiratory Care.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for your accredited respiratory therapy program to send official transcripts directly to the Kentucky Board of Respiratory Care. Transcripts must verify your graduation and program completion.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow the specific instructions provided by the Board within the Kentucky Online Gateway to complete the required state and federal criminal background check. This typically involves fingerprinting.

steps.title: Provide NBRC Examination Scores
steps.description: Ensure the Board can verify your passing scores on the NBRC examination. This may involve having the NBRC send official score reports directly to the Kentucky Board of Respiratory Care or providing information for the board to look them up.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Kentucky respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Kentucky have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Kentucky. To work in Kentucky, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Kentucky?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Kentucky while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Kentucky
hero.dek: To apply for Respiratory Therapist licensure by endorsement in Kentucky, you must create an account and submit your application through the Kentucky Online Gateway (KOG) portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to gateway.ky.gov and click 'Register' or 'Create an Account'. Follow the prompts to enter your personal information, create a username and password, and confirm your account. Once registered, you can log in to access the Board of Respiratory Care application.
